2019-01-24.title: The Crisis in Venezuela Was Years in the Making. Here’s How It Happened.
2019-01-24.abstract: For years, Venezuela’s economy has slowly collapsed, fueling poverty, crime and mass demonstrations. The New York Times has documented that descent, and how Nicolás Maduro has stayed in power.

2019-01-24.title: U.S. Releases American Journalist Who Works for Iranian TV After Testimony
2019-01-24.abstract: The journalist, Marzieh Hashemi, an anchorwoman for Iran’s PressTV, had been under arrest since Jan. 13, as a material witness for an undisclosed federal investigation.

2019-01-24.title: Christian Politician in Indonesia Is Freed After Blasphemy Prison Term
2019-01-24.abstract: After serving his sentence for blasphemy against Islam, a former Jakarta governor finds that religion still roils Indonesian politics.

2019-01-25.title: Australia’s Old and New Spending Spree?
2019-01-25.abstract: This week’s newsletter looks at money and priorities in the land of the long boom. Plus: Free tickets to Fringe World in Perth!

2019-01-25.title: Echoes of the Past in Venezuela Crisis, but Heard More Lightly
2019-01-25.abstract: The United States’ recognition of Venezuela’s opposition leader Juan Guaidó as interim president seemed to follow a pattern familiar to Latin America.

2019-01-25.title: Everest Fraud Leads Insurers to Threaten a Boycott
2019-01-25.abstract: An elaborate scheme centered on helicopter rescues from Everest and nearby peaks has defrauded insurance companies of millions of dollars.

2019-01-25.title: Explosions Shake Shopping District in Changchun, China
2019-01-25.abstract: The blasts in an underground parking lot and an apartment building in the northeastern city sent people running. At least one was killed, the authorities said.

2019-01-25.title: U.N. Execution Expert Will Investigate Khashoggi Killing
2019-01-25.abstract: The inquiry marks the first United Nations involvement in the case, but it falls short of the full, formal investigation demanded by Turkey.

2019-01-26.title: Within Venezuelan Military Ranks, a Struggle Over What Leader to Back
2019-01-26.abstract: The leadership publicly swore allegiance to the nation’s president, Nicolás Maduro, but interviews show a more complicated, ongoing conflict in the rank and file.

2019-01-26.title: Mexico Protests U.S. Decision to Return Asylum Seekers
2019-01-26.abstract: The Mexican government said that it disagreed with “this unilateral measure” but that it would take in people who are applying for asylum in the United States.

2019-01-26.title: Arrests in Terror Plot Raise Questions About Canada’s Refugee Embrace
2019-01-26.abstract: The police offered few details about what they called a terrorist bomb plot, or about the two men, one a Syrian refugee, whom they said had planned it.

2019-01-26.title: ‘Individual Plaintiff’ Brought Case Against Imprisoned Navy Veteran, Iran Says
2019-01-26.abstract: An Iranian news report deepened the mystery over why the veteran, Michael R. White, was imprisoned in Iran last July. His mother said she feared he has cancer.

2019-01-26.title: Body of Julen Roselló, 2-Year-Old Boy Trapped in a Well, Is Found in Spain
2019-01-26.abstract: The discovery brought a grim end to a massive search-and-rescue mission that had gripped Spain for two weeks.

2019-01-27.title: Philippines Cathedral Bombing Kills 20
2019-01-27.abstract: Scores more were wounded in twin blasts in a predominantly Muslim area of the southern Philippines where the government has been fighting Islamist separatists.

2019-01-27.title: Prince Philip Says He’s ‘Deeply Sorry’ About Car Crash
2019-01-27.abstract: The 97-year-old Duke of Edinburgh apologized in a letter to a woman who sustained a broken wrist in the accident, British news outlets reported.

2019-01-27.title: Quest to Solve Assassination Mystery Revives an AIDS Conspiracy Theory
2019-01-27.abstract: In a new documentary, a former South African militia member says his group used phony vaccinations to spread H.I.V. among blacks. Experts are deeply skeptical.

2019-01-27.title: Too Ugly to Be Saved? Singapore Weighs Fate of Its Brutalist Buildings
2019-01-27.abstract: A few landmarks of so-called Brutalist architecture are on the verge of being sold to private developers, prompting a last-ditch scramble by enthusiasts to have the buildings protected.

2019-01-27.title: Another Side of #MeToo: Male Managers Fearful of Mentoring Women
2019-01-27.abstract: At the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, some male leaders said they were avoiding one-on-one time with junior female colleagues, a trend that could deprive women of valuable exposure.

2019-01-28.title: As 2nd Brazil Dam Threatens to Collapse, Death Toll Rises to 58
2019-01-28.abstract: Two days after a dam break left at least 58 dead, residents of Brumadinho are forced to evacuate again, while experts warn that the regulatory system is flawed.

2019-01-28.title: A Thai Singer Wore a Swastika. Was It Prejudice or Ignorance?
2019-01-28.abstract: The episode is one of several Nazi-related controversies to crop up in Asia, where Holocaust awareness is patchy, swastikas resemble an ancient symbol, and some see Hitler as a model of strength.

2019-01-28.title: How Much Warmer Was Your City in 2018?
2019-01-28.abstract: Interactive chart showing how much warmer or cooler it was in 2018 in more than 3,800 cities around the world.

2019-01-28.title: Abortion, Newly Legal in Ireland, Faces Old Roadblocks
2019-01-28.abstract: Women say the entire process is still hush-hush, given an emboldened anti-abortion movement and a continuing stigma that has left doctors slow to sign up for the service.

2019-01-28.title: Chinese Rights Lawyer Swept Up in Xi’s Crackdown Gets More Than 4 Years in Prison
2019-01-28.abstract: Wang Quanzhang, an outspoken rights advocate, had been in custody since 2015 under President Xi Jinping’s campaign to obliterate threats to Communist Party control.

2019-01-29.title: ‘World’s Loneliest Duck’ Dies on Tiny Pacific Island That Loved Him
2019-01-29.abstract: His nickname was a little off, since Trevor, the only duck on the island of Niue, was beloved by its 1,600 residents. They mourned after he was killed by a stray dog.

2019-01-29.title: Our Dreams of Peace: Envisioning an End to War in Afghanistan
2019-01-29.abstract: Two New York Times reporters from Afghanistan find themselves suddenly dreaming of what they will do if peace comes — as are their friends, and even some Taliban.

2019-01-29.title: Pakistani Woman Accused of Blasphemy Will Remain Free, Supreme Court Rules
2019-01-29.abstract: A three-member panel dismissed a challenge to the top court’s October decision to free Asia Bibi, a Christian farmworker who had spent eight years on death row.

2019-01-29.title: In Critical Brexit Vote, Theresa May Survives
2019-01-29.abstract: Lawmakers narrowly rejected an amendment that would have put Parliament in charge of the withdrawal from the E.U., overturning constitutional protocol.

2019-01-29.title: Data Breaches Dent Singapore’s Image as a Tech Innovator
2019-01-29.abstract: Experts say security concerns are a pitfall for countries that are pushing to make vast troves of data more accessible and centralized.

2019-01-30.title: Opposition Leader, and Oil, Become Focus of Venezuela-U.S. Struggle
2019-01-30.abstract: After Washington handed control of Venezuela’s assets in the United States to the opposition leader, the government of Nicolás Maduro struck back fiercely.

2019-01-30.title: ‘Along the Main Road You See the Graves’: U.N. Says Hundreds Killed in Congo
2019-01-30.abstract: United Nations officials attributed three days of horrific violence last month in a northwestern region of the Democratic Republic of Congo to strife between ethnic groups.

2019-01-30.title: Grenade Attack on Philippine Mosque Kills Two
2019-01-30.abstract: The attack in the city of Zamboanga was the second in days on a place of worship in the Philippines’ restive south. A cathedral bombing on Sunday killed 21.

2019-01-30.title: ‘It’s Entirely Up to Me’: Indigenous Australians Find Empowerment in Start-Ups
2019-01-30.abstract: Indigenous entrepreneurs say that the field is an alluring, empowering avenue to take their destiny in into their own hands.

2019-01-30.title: Maduro Sounds Conciliatory but Warns: U.S. Intervention Would Be Worse Than Vietnam
2019-01-30.abstract: The Venezuelan leader said he was ready for talks with the opposition. He also said a U.S. invasion would give America a Vietnam nightmare.

2019-01-31.title: Maduro Turns to Special Police Force to Crush Dissent
2019-01-31.abstract: Facing the biggest challenge yet to his rule, President Nicolás Maduro of Venezuela has called on a special police force to serve as enforcers where he once relied on the military.

2019-01-31.title: 6 Men Convicted in 1982 Murder of Chilean Leader
2019-01-31.abstract: A judge found that a former security agent, four doctors and an aide conspired to poison Eduardo Frei, the leader of the moderate opposition against the dictator Augusto Pinochet.

2019-01-31.title: Could You Last 11 Days Without the Internet? Tonga Finds Out the Hard Way
2019-01-31.abstract: Since a fiber-optic cable was severed on Jan. 20, contact with the outside world has been difficult for residents of the remote island nation.

2019-01-31.title: Afghan Government Control Over Country Falters, U.S. Report Says
2019-01-31.abstract: The Afghan government’s control of its country declined last year, in terms of both territory and population, according to a United States government report.

2019-01-31.title: New Zealand Vowed 100,000 New Homes to Ease Crunch. So Far It Has Built 47.
2019-01-31.abstract: Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern’s government said it would scrap its initial target of 1,000 homes by July, while a study showed that housing had grown more unaffordable.
